title.en: Crowded Sub-GHz: The IoT Technologies That Must Coexist
title.zh: 拥挤的 Sub-GHz：必须共存的物联网技术

blocks.en: The radio spectrum below 1 GHz ("sub-1 GHz" or S1G) is prized for the Internet of Things (IoT): low-frequency signals travel far and punch through walls, which is exactly what battery-powered sensors need. The catch is that this band is unlicensed ("license-exempt") — anyone may transmit, with no central referee. So many unrelated radio systems end up sharing the same airwaves without coordinating. IEEE Std 802.19.3-2021 is a *recommended practice* that tackles this problem. Its scope (clause 1.1) is narrow and deliberate: it gives guidance on how to implement, configure, and commission systems that share spectrum between IEEE Std 802.11ah (Wi-Fi HaLow) and the IEEE Std 802.15.4 smart utility networking (SUN) frequency shift keying (FSK) physical layer (PHY) in the sub-1 GHz bands.
blocks.zh: 1 GHz 以下的无线频谱（即 "sub-1 GHz" 或 S1G）在物联网（IoT）中备受青睐：低频信号传播距离远、穿墙能力强，这正是电池供电的传感器所需要的。问题在于，这一频段是免许可的（"license-exempt"），任何人都可以发射信号，没有中央裁判。于是大量互不相关的无线系统最终在同一片空域中共享频谱，却彼此不协调。IEEE Std 802.19.3-2021 是一份解决该问题的 *推荐规程（recommended practice）*。它的范围（第 1.1 条）有意地划得很窄：它针对在 sub-1 GHz 频段中共享频谱的系统，即 IEEE Std 802.11ah（Wi-Fi HaLow）与 IEEE Std 802.15.4 智能公用事业网络（SUN）频移键控（FSK）物理层（PHY），给出实现、配置与开通方面的指导。

blocks.en: A "recommended practice" is not a hard standard. Throughout the document the words carry precise weight (clause 1.2, Word usage): *shall* = mandatory, *should* = recommended but not required, *may* = permitted, *can* = able to. So when 802.19.3 says a device *should* do something for coexistence, that is advice you are strongly encouraged to follow — not a conformance requirement.
blocks.zh: "推荐规程" 不是强制标准。在整份文档中，这些词都有精确含义（第 1.2 条，用词约定）：*shall（应当）* = 强制要求，*should（宜）* = 建议但非必须，*may（可）* = 允许，*can（能够）* = 具备能力。因此当 802.19.3 说某设备为共存 *宜（should）* 做某事时，那是强烈建议你遵循的忠告，而非合规要求。

blocks.en: Before you can manage coexistence, you need shared vocabulary. Clause 3.1 defines the key terms. **Coexistence** is the ability of multiple systems to operate at the same time, in the same physical space, and in the same or overlapping frequency bands — even when they use different sets of rules. A **coexistence mechanism** is any means to improve performance, resilience, and reliability when those systems run simultaneously. **Interference** is unwanted power entering a channel that disrupts reception. Other defined terms set up later chapters: *beamforming*, *duty cycle* (the fraction of time a device actually transmits), *smart utility network (SUN)*, *restricted access window (RAW)* and *target wake time (TWT)* from Wi-Fi HaLow, and *common signaling mode*. Clause 3.2 then lists the acronym soup you will meet — AP, PANC, CSMA/CA, TDMA, ALOHA, OFDM, FSK, LPWAN, S1G, and more.
blocks.zh: 在管理共存之前，需要先建立共同的术语。第 3.1 条定义了关键术语。**共存（coexistence）** 指多个系统能够在同一时间、同一物理空间、同一或重叠频段中运行的能力——即便它们使用不同的规则集。**共存机制（coexistence mechanism）** 是指当这些系统同时运行时，用以提升性能、韧性与可靠性的任何手段。**干扰（interference）** 是指进入信道、扰乱接收的非期望功率。其他定义术语为后续章节作铺垫：*波束成形（beamforming）*、*占空比（duty cycle，设备实际发射时间的占比）*、*智能公用事业网络（SUN）*、来自 Wi-Fi HaLow 的 *受限接入窗口（RAW）* 与 *目标唤醒时间（TWT）*，以及 *公共信令模式（common signaling mode）*。第 3.2 条随后列出了你将遇到的一大堆缩写——AP、PANC、CSMA/CA、TDMA、ALOHA、OFDM、FSK、LPWAN、S1G 等等。

blocks.en: Clause 4 surveys the cast of characters. Its focus (clause 4.1, Introduction) is coexistence between 802.11ah and 802.15.4g, but it paints the bigger picture by also describing LoRa, Sigfox, and others likely to be found in the same bands. All of them serve IoT needs — low bandwidth, long distance, low power — using S1G. They differ in topology and even in what they call the network coordinator: an *access point (AP)* for 802.11ah, a *PAN coordinator (PANC)* for 802.15.4g/4w, a *gateway* for LoRa, and a *base station* for Sigfox. Ranges vary too: 802.11ah and 802.15.4g reach about 1 km, while the LPWAN technologies (802.15.4w, LoRa, Sigfox) reach up to 15 km.
blocks.zh: 第 4 条对登场角色进行了梳理。它的重点（第 4.1 条，引言）是 802.11ah 与 802.15.4g 之间的共存，但通过同时描述 LoRa、Sigfox 等可能出现在相同频段的系统，勾勒出更完整的图景。它们都服务于物联网需求——低带宽、远距离、低功耗——并使用 S1G 频段。它们在拓扑结构上各不相同，甚至对网络协调设备的称呼也不同：802.11ah 称之为 *接入点（AP）*，802.15.4g/4w 称之为 *PAN 协调器（PANC）*，LoRa 称之为 *网关（gateway）*，Sigfox 称之为 *基站（base station）*。覆盖范围也各异：802.11ah 与 802.15.4g 约达 1 km，而 LPWAN 技术（802.15.4w、LoRa、Sigfox）可达 15 km。

blocks.svg: <svg viewBox="0 0 460 200" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.5"><rect x="10" y="10" width="440" height="180" rx="6"/><text x="230" y="30" text-anchor="middle" stroke="none" fill="currentColor" font-size="13">Shared sub-1 GHz license-exempt band (e.g. 902-928 MHz)</text><line x1="30" y1="160" x2="430" y2="160"/><g font-size="10" stroke="none" fill="currentColor" text-anchor="middle"><rect x="35" y="60" width="70" height="90" rx="4" fill="none" stroke="currentColor"/><text x="70" y="55">802.11ah</text><text x="70" y="105">OFDM</text><text x="70" y="120">~1 km</text><rect x="120" y="80" width="70" height="70" rx="4" fill="none" stroke="currentColor"/><text x="155" y="75">802.15.4g</text><text x="155" y="115">FSK/OFDM</text><text x="155" y="130">~1 km</text><rect x="205" y="100" width="70" height="50" rx="4" fill="none" stroke="currentColor"/><text x="240" y="95">802.15.4w</text><text x="240" y="130">~15 km</text><rect x="290" y="100" width="60" height="50" rx="4" fill="none" stroke="currentColor"/><text x="320" y="95">LoRa</text><text x="320" y="130">CSS</text><rect x="365" y="110" width="55" height="40" rx="4" fill="none" stroke="currentColor"/><text x="392" y="105">Sigfox</text><text x="392" y="135">UNB</text></g><text x="230" y="180" text-anchor="middle" stroke="none" fill="currentColor" font-size="10">No coordination between systems — they must coexist</text></svg>

caption.en: Many unrelated IoT systems pile into the same unlicensed sub-1 GHz band, each with its own modulation, range, and channel-access style. Wider boxes hint at wider channels; none of them coordinates with the others.
caption.zh: 众多互不相关的物联网系统挤入同一免许可的 sub-1 GHz 频段，各自有不同的调制方式、覆盖范围与信道接入方式。框越宽暗示信道越宽；它们之间都不互相协调。

blocks.en: The two stars of the show get the most ink. **IEEE Std 802.11ah / Wi-Fi HaLow (clause 4.2)** is an OFDM PHY with 1 MHz minimum channel spacing (and optional 2/4/8/16 MHz channels), reaching 150 kb/s at 1 km up to hundreds of Mb/s at short range. To support huge device counts it extends the association identifier (AID) up to 8191 stations per AP in a four-level page/block/sub-block/station hierarchy. Channel access uses CSMA/CA (carrier sense multiple access with collision avoidance) via EDCA. Two HaLow features matter for coexistence: the **restricted access window (RAW)**, which clusters stations into time slots — effectively blending CSMA/CA and TDMA — and **subchannel selective transmission (SST)**, which lets stations hop to a cleaner subchannel to dodge fading or interference. **IEEE Std 802.15.4g (clause 4.3)** is the smart-utility workhorse: a PHY amendment defining SUN-FSK, SUN-OFDM, and SUN-O-QPSK, with very narrow channels (down to 12.5 kHz spacing) and 6.25 kb/s–2.4 Mb/s rates. It uses slotted or unslotted CSMA/CA, plus timeslotted channel hopping (TSCH) for deterministic, interference-resilient access, in star or mesh topologies.
blocks.zh: 两位主角着墨最多。**IEEE Std 802.11ah / Wi-Fi HaLow（第 4.2 条）** 采用 OFDM 物理层，最小信道间隔为 1 MHz（可选 2/4/8/16 MHz 信道），在 1 km 处可达 150 kb/s，短距离下可达数百 Mb/s。为支持海量设备，它将关联标识符（AID）扩展到每个 AP 最多 8191 个站点，并采用页/块/子块/站点四级层次结构。信道接入通过 EDCA 使用 CSMA/CA（载波侦听多址/冲突避免）。两个 HaLow 特性对共存尤为重要：**受限接入窗口（RAW）** 将站点归入时隙——实质上融合了 CSMA/CA 与 TDMA；**子信道选择性发送（SST）** 让站点跳到更干净的子信道，以躲避衰落或干扰。**IEEE Std 802.15.4g（第 4.3 条）** 是智能公用事业的主力：它是一项物理层修正，定义了 SUN-FSK、SUN-OFDM 和 SUN-O-QPSK，信道极窄（间隔可低至 12.5 kHz），速率为 6.25 kb/s–2.4 Mb/s。它在星型或网状拓扑中使用时隙化或非时隙化 CSMA/CA，并辅以时隙信道跳变（TSCH）以实现确定性、抗干扰的接入。

blocks.en: The remaining systems are low power wide area network (LPWAN) technologies that trade data rate for extreme range and battery life. **IEEE Std 802.15.4w (clause 4.4)** is an LPWAN extension to the 802.15.4 LECIM PHY, covering 10–15 km cells using GMSK at roughly 600 b/s–9 kb/s (effective 60–900 b/s after coding and fragmentation), with TDMA or ALOHA access. **LoRa (clause 4.5)** is a proprietary PHY using chirp spread spectrum (CSS), which shrugs off oscillator frequency offsets; the LoRaWAN MAC on top defines Class A (uplink-triggered receive windows, ALOHA), Class B (scheduled beacons), and Class C (always listening) devices. **Sigfox (clause 4.6)** is an ultra-narrow-band system — 100 Hz–600 Hz uplink channels — using BPSK uplink and GFSK downlink with a fixed 12-byte payload, pure random access, and time-and-frequency diversity (a message plus two replicas on different frequencies and times).
blocks.zh: 其余系统都是低功耗广域网（LPWAN）技术，以牺牲数据速率换取极远覆盖与超长电池寿命。**IEEE Std 802.15.4w（第 4.4 条）** 是对 802.15.4 LECIM 物理层的 LPWAN 扩展，使用 GMSK 调制覆盖 10–15 km 小区，速率约为 600 b/s–9 kb/s（编码与分片后有效速率为 60–900 b/s），采用 TDMA 或 ALOHA 接入。**LoRa（第 4.5 条）** 是一种专有物理层，采用线性调频扩频（CSS），能很好地抵御振荡器频偏；其上层的 LoRaWAN MAC 定义了 Class A（由上行触发接收窗口，ALOHA）、Class B（定时信标）和 Class C（始终侦听）设备。**Sigfox（第 4.6 条）** 是一种超窄带系统——上行信道仅 100 Hz–600 Hz——上行使用 BPSK、下行使用 GFSK，载荷固定为 12 字节，采用纯随机接入，并使用时频分集（一条消息加上在不同频率与时间上的两个副本）。

blocks.en:
flowchart TD
  S[ETSI TS 103 357 / Low Throughput Networks] --> A[4.7.1 Lfour family]
  S --> B[4.7.2 TS-UNB family]
  S --> C[4.7.3 DD-UNB family]
  A --> A1[Uplink only / chirp BPSK / rate-1/4 LDPC]
  B --> B1[Bidirectional / MSK + frequency hopping]
  C --> C1[Bidirectional / binary FSK + BCH + hopping]
blocks.zh:
flowchart TD
  S[ETSI TS 103 357 / 低吞吐网络] --> A[4.7.1 Lfour 族]
  S --> B[4.7.2 TS-UNB 族]
  S --> C[4.7.3 DD-UNB 族]
  A --> A1[仅上行 / 啁啾 BPSK / 1/4 码率 LDPC]
  B --> B1[双向 / MSK + 跳频]
  C --> C1[双向 / 二进制 FSK + BCH + 跳频]

blocks.en: Clause 4.7 adds three more European ultra-narrow-band (UNB) families standardized in ETSI TS 103 357 as Low Throughput Networks. The **Lfour family (4.7.1)** offers uplink only, using chirp-modulated BPSK over 50–160 kHz with a rate-1/4 LDPC code (identical to the 802.15.4w code) and a 150–155 dB maximum coupling loss (MCL — the largest attenuation a link can tolerate). The **TS-UNB family (4.7.2)** is bidirectional, using MSK at 2.3 kS/s with frequency hopping and a rate-1/3 convolutional code that spreads data across bursts on different frequencies. The **DD-UNB family (4.7.3)** is bidirectional too, using binary FSK at 500 S/s with BCH coding, frequency hopping, and optional relay links for orphan endpoints. Clause 4.8 (Summary) collapses all of this into one comparison table covering each technology's PHY modulation, channel width, data rate, range, max transmit power, and channel-access method — a handy cheat sheet for spotting where these radios might collide.
blocks.zh: 第 4.7 条又引入了三种由 ETSI TS 103 357 标准化的欧洲超窄带（UNB）族，统称低吞吐网络。**Lfour 族（4.7.1）** 仅提供上行，在 50–160 kHz 上使用啁啾调制 BPSK，采用 1/4 码率 LDPC 码（与 802.15.4w 的编码相同），最大耦合损耗（MCL，即链路所能容忍的最大衰减）为 150–155 dB。**TS-UNB 族（4.7.2）** 为双向，使用 2.3 kS/s 的 MSK 配合跳频，并采用 1/3 码率卷积码，将数据分散到不同频率的多个突发上。**DD-UNB 族（4.7.3）** 同样为双向，使用 500 S/s 的二进制 FSK 配合 BCH 编码、跳频，并为孤立端点提供可选的中继链路。第 4.8 条（小结）将以上内容浓缩为一张对比表，涵盖每种技术的物理层调制、信道宽度、数据速率、覆盖范围、最大发射功率与信道接入方式——是判断这些电台可能在何处发生碰撞的实用速查表。

blocks.en: Clause 5 turns from *what* these technologies are to *where* they are used — and that is the real coexistence trigger. Its introduction (clause 5.1) makes the key point: all of these serve overlapping IoT domains (smart utility, smart city, field monitoring, building automation), so the *same physical place* often hosts several of them at once, which is exactly why they must coexist. For the LPWAN systems (802.15.4w, LoRa, Sigfox), traffic is highly asymmetrical — mostly uplink monitoring. The following subclauses give each technology's representative use cases; collectively they are this lesson's coverage map.
blocks.zh: 第 5 条从这些技术 *是什么* 转向它们 *用在哪里*——而这正是共存问题的真正触发点。其引言（第 5.1 条）点明了关键：所有这些技术都服务于相互重叠的物联网领域（智能公用事业、智慧城市、现场监测、楼宇自动化），因此 *同一物理场所* 往往同时容纳多种技术，这正是它们必须共存的原因。对于 LPWAN 系统（802.15.4w、LoRa、Sigfox），流量高度不对称——以上行监测为主。接下来的各小节给出了每种技术的代表性用例；它们合起来就是本课的覆盖图。

summary.en: Coverage map: the per-technology use cases (clauses 5.2-5.6)
summary.zh: 覆盖图：各技术的用例（第 5.2–5.6 条）

blocks.en:
**5.2 IEEE Std 802.11ah use cases** — Not yet widely deployed, but promoted as Wi-Fi HaLow (Japan formed the 802.11ah Promotion Council, AHPC). Use cases span smart home/building (automation, appliances, security, wearables, health), smart power (smart grid, smart meter, smart lighting), backhaul (bridging/mesh, sensor-network backbone, drone backup, hot spots), monitoring (factory inspection, wildlife, infrastructure vibration sensors), smart city (surveillance with edge computing, water-pipe management, public transport, ITS), and industry. These mix indoor/outdoor, low/high throughput, and few/thousands of devices.

**5.3 IEEE Std 802.15.4g use cases** — Originally for smart metering; the Wi-SUN Alliance built specs on it and millions of devices are deployed (Japan alone schedules 65M+ smart meters). Use cases: smart utility (AMI, peak-load management, distribution automation, EV charging, gas/water metering, leak detection), smart city (street lighting, parking, traffic, environmental sensing), smart home (thermostats, HVAC, energy displays, health), machine-to-machine (agriculture, structural health monitoring, asset management), and industrial plant monitoring.

**5.4 LoRa use cases** — Two buckets: smart city (smart lighting, air-quality/pollution monitoring, parking and vehicle management, facilities/infrastructure, fire and waste management) and industrial (radiation/leak detection, smart sensors, item location/tracking, shipping and transportation).

**5.5 Sigfox use cases** — Supply chain/logistics and retail; smart cities (lighting, public transport, utilities/energy, smart buildings and security); monitoring (agriculture/environment, home and lifestyle, service/vehicle monitoring, road/structure sensors); and industry (manufacturing).

**5.6 IEEE Std 802.15.4w use cases** — The standard states plainly that 802.15.4w can be applied to all use cases for LoRa and Sigfox, underscoring just how much overlap exists.

This deepdive consolidates the survey units 4.7.1, 4.7.2, 4.7.3, 4.8 and all of clause 5 alongside the prose above; the scope (1.1), word usage (1.2), definitions (3.1), and acronyms (3.2) are taught in the opening blocks, and the technology overviews (4.1-4.6) in the middle blocks.
blocks.zh:
**5.2 IEEE Std 802.11ah 用例** — 尚未广泛部署，但以 Wi-Fi HaLow 之名推广（日本成立了 802.11ah 推进委员会 AHPC）。用例涵盖智能家居/楼宇（自动化、智能家电、安防、可穿戴、健康）、智能电力（智能电网、智能电表、智能照明）、回传（桥接/网状、传感网骨干、无人机备份网络、热点）、监测（工厂巡检、野生动物、基础设施振动传感器）、智慧城市（结合边缘计算的监控、水管管理、公共交通、ITS）以及工业。这些用例混合了室内/室外、低/高吞吐、少量/数千台设备。

**5.3 IEEE Std 802.15.4g 用例** — 最初用于智能抄表；Wi-SUN 联盟在其上构建规范，已部署数百万台设备（仅日本就计划部署超 6500 万台智能电表）。用例：智能公用事业（AMI、峰值负荷管理、配电自动化、电动车充电、燃气/水表、泄漏检测）、智慧城市（路灯、停车、交通、环境感知）、智能家居（恒温器、暖通空调、能耗显示、健康）、机器对机器（农业、结构健康监测、资产管理）以及工业厂区监测。

**5.4 LoRa 用例** — 两大类：智慧城市（智能照明、空气质量/污染监测、停车与车辆管理、设施/基础设施、火灾与废物管理）和工业（辐射/泄漏检测、智能传感器、物品定位/追踪、运输与物流）。

**5.5 Sigfox 用例** — 供应链/物流与零售；智慧城市（照明、公共交通、公用事业/能源、智能楼宇与安防）；监测（农业/环境、家居与生活、服务/车辆监测、道路/结构传感器）；以及工业（制造）。

**5.6 IEEE Std 802.15.4w 用例** — 标准直接指出，802.15.4w 可应用于 LoRa 和 Sigfox 的所有用例，凸显了二者之间巨大的重叠。

本深入解读将综述单元 4.7.1、4.7.2、4.7.3、4.8 及整个第 5 条与上文正文整合在一起；范围（1.1）、用词约定（1.2）、定义（3.1）与缩写（3.2）在开篇各块中讲授，技术概述（4.1–4.6）在中间各块中讲授。

blocks.en: Notice the pattern: smart meters, street lighting, parking, environmental sensing, and asset tracking show up in nearly every technology's use-case list. That overlap is the whole reason 802.19.3 exists — when an 802.11ah hot spot, a Wi-SUN mesh, and a LoRa sensor network all blanket the same neighborhood in the 902-928 MHz band, none can assume the air is theirs alone.
blocks.zh: 请注意这一规律：智能电表、路灯、停车、环境感知和资产追踪几乎出现在每种技术的用例清单中。正是这种重叠构成了 802.19.3 存在的全部理由——当一个 802.11ah 热点、一张 Wi-SUN 网状网和一个 LoRa 传感网都覆盖在 902–928 MHz 频段的同一片区域时，谁都不能假定这片空域只属于自己。

cites.note: Clause 1 (Scope, Word usage), Clause 3 (Definitions, acronyms), Clause 4 (technology overviews 4.1-4.8), and Clause 5 (use cases 5.1-5.6).
